notes: Strong alignment — 10,832 open-text responses across 4 questions. Public widely discussed talent retention, compute access, and graduate funding.
Near-perfect alignment. Both public and experts prioritized sovereign compute capacity and open data commons.
Highest response volume of any pillar — 23,303 responses across 10 questions. Public engagement was intense on SME support and IP retention.
keyTension: Public actually rated commercialization high (9.1) — contrary to narrative. The tension is about pace vs. safety guardrails, not opposition to commercialization itself.

notes: Regulation co-occurs heavily with trust questions. Real data shows public and experts are largely aligned — both want AIDA-style risk-tiered frameworks.
keyTension: Divergence is on enforcement teeth, not the need for regulation. Public wants stronger penalties; industry experts favor compliance-first approaches.

notes: Lowest response volume. Public discusses it in context of competitiveness ('learn from the US/EU') more than multilateral governance.
keyTension: Experts focus on G7/OECD governance frameworks; public focuses on avoiding regulatory arbitrage with the EU AI Act.

notes: Trust and regulation questions overlap heavily. 82.8% of respondents with text mentioned trust-related keywords — the highest of any pillar.
keyTension: Public wants reactive redress mechanisms; Task Force experts emphasize proactive auditing and transparency registries.

notes: Real data inverts the assumed narrative: experts actually score inclusion HIGHER than the public keyword analysis suggests (25% mention rate vs. 82% for trust).
keyTension: Inclusion terms (Indigenous, equity, accessibility) appear in only 25% of responses — far below trust (83%) and adoption (82%). The public engagement gap on inclusion is the real story.

notes: Near-perfect alignment. Cybersecurity and foreign acquisition concerns appear consistently across age groups and sectors.
